question: Looking at the US GDP map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: Massachusetts and Wyoming have similar GDPs.
options.B: The GDP differences between states cannot be compared at all from this map.
options.C: The GDP gap between Massachusetts and Wyoming might exceed that between Kansas and Vermont.
options.D: Kansas and Vermont clearly have a larger GDP gap than any other pair of highlighted states.

explanation: Massachusetts (163.5$B) and Wyoming (265.9$B) have a 102.4$B GDP difference, while Kansas (29.6$B) and Vermont (30.4$B) have only a 0.8$B difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Massachusetts and Wyoming into the same color bin (150-300$B range), making their large difference appear similar, while Kansas and Vermont appear more different due to different color bins.
